title: Elaphoglossum wawrae
intro: Elaphoglossum wawrae, also known as Wawra's elaphoglossum, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and skin conditions, due to its antinociceptive and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum wawrae include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum wawrae is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory disorders.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum wawrae belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family. This species is further classified as a genus within the Polypodiopsida class of ferns. Elaphoglossum wawrae specifically falls under the Polypodiopsida subclass and the Polypodiales order. Within the order, it is classified as a member of the Elaphoglossum genus and the Elaphoglossum section. Elaphoglossum wawrae is a species of epiphytic fern.
